Class ObjectVal

java.lang.Object
de.uka.ilkd.key.smt.model.ObjectVal
public class ObjectVal extends Object
Represents an object inside a heap.

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    ObjectVal(String name)
    creates a new object value of the given name

      public ObjectVal(String name)
      creates a new object value of the given name

  • Method Details

    • putFunValue

      public void putFunValue(String fun, String val)
      associate the function name with the given value
      Parameters:
      fun - the name of the function
      val - the value to be associated with the specified function

    • setArrayValue

      public void setArrayValue(int i, String val)
      set the i-th array element to the given value
      Parameters:
      i - the array index
      val - the value

    • getArrayValue

      public String getArrayValue(int i)
      returns the i-th array element
      Parameters:
      i - the index of the array element to be returned
      Returns:
      the value stores at index i

      public void setExactInstance(boolean exactInstance)
      sets the (exact) dynamic type of this object

      exactInstance - the exactInstance to set

    • isExactInstance

      public boolean isExactInstance()
      queries whether the (exact) dynamic type of this object is known

    • getArrayValues

      public Map<Integer,String> getArrayValues()
      returns all values stored in this array
      Returns:
      map associating an index with the value stored at that index

      returns the associated values for the given function names

      map of function name to associated value

    • get

      public String get(String field)
      returns the currently associated valut to the specified field

    • put

      public String put(String field, String value)
      associated a field to the given value
      Parameters:
      field - the field
      value - the value assigned to the specified field
      Returns:
      the old value of the field (or null if no value was known before)

      Returns:
      the value associated to the field (or null if not known)
